items.title: Codex-exec runner for the onboarding runtime follow-on

prompt: Work item: uaa-0022 — Codex-exec runner for the onboarding runtime follow-on.
Goal: replace the current manual translation of the operator-guide runtime lane with a bounded Codex execution recipe that starts after `onboard-agent` and `scaffold-wrapper-crate`.
Key decisions already made:
- Default baseline is `opencode`-level support.
- `gemini_cli` is a minimal exception path only and requires explicit justification.
- `codex` and `claude_code` remain feature-rich references for optional richer surfaces.
Steps:
1) Define the runtime-lane input contract: which packet files, registry fields, and scaffold outputs the runner reads.
2) Define the output contract: runtime code, wrapper coverage source-of-truth, committed manifest evidence, and an achieved-tier summary.
3) Define the instruction recipe so Codex defaults to the `opencode` template and only pulls in `gemini_cli` / `codex` / `claude_code` patterns when justified.
4) Define the review requirement for `minimal` outcomes: explicit justification plus follow-up work to reach `default`.
Output: a repo-owned runner plan ready for implementation, without reopening the operator guide or folding in publication-refresh follow-on work.
deliverables: Pinned tiering policy for default/minimal/feature-rich runtime implementations
Implementation-ready execution recipe for Codex to complete the runtime follow-on

notes: This is the next create-mode automation seam after the recommendation lane and M6 control-plane boundary.
The runner should start from the operator-guide runtime follow-on, not from an open-ended request to 'copy existing backends'.

Default means `opencode`-level support. Minimal means `gemini_cli`-level support and requires explicit justification. Feature-rich means selectively borrowing richer patterns from `codex` and `claude_code`.
Keep publication refresh automation after runtime evidence exists as a separate follow-up milestone.

Implemented on 2026-04-29: `xtask runtime-follow-on` landed, the create-mode operator guide now uses the bounded runtime packet, and the runner emits a validated handoff into the publication and green gates.

items.title: Tooling/spec updates for agent_api.session bucket

prompt: Work item: uaa-0013 — Tooling/spec updates for agent_api.session bucket.
Goal: introduce agent_api.session.* as a first-class capability bucket (rubric + tooling) so onboarding and audits can bucket session capabilities mechanically.
Steps:
1) Update docs/specs/unified-agent-api/capabilities-schema-spec.md to include agent_api.session.* in the bucket rubric list (near agent_api.exec.*).
2) Update crates/xtask/src/capability_matrix.rs: bucket_for(...) should recognize agent_api.session.* and bucket_order(...) should include agent_api.session in the canonical bucket ordering.
3) Run the generators/audits: `cargo run -p xtask -- capability-matrix` and `cargo run -p xtask -- capability-matrix-audit`.
Output: PR-ready spec + tooling changes; no backend behavior changes.
deliverables: Updated bucket rubric + xtask bucketing for agent_api.session.*

notes: Update docs/specs/unified-agent-api/capabilities-schema-spec.md rubric to include agent_api.session.*.
Update crates/xtask/src/capability_matrix.rs bucket_for(...) and bucket_order(...) to recognize agent_api.session.* so the generated matrix groups session capabilities correctly.

Confirm crates/xtask/src/capability_matrix_audit.rs behavior once any agent_api.session.* capabilities are added (audit requires new agent_api.* capabilities to be supported by >=2 built-in backends unless allowlisted).

items.title: Obs facet schema (events + completion)

prompt: Work item: uaa-0014 — Obs facet schema (events + completion).
Goal: define a stable, bounded obs metadata schema that can carry run_id / trace_context / tags across AgentWrapperEvent.data and AgentWrapperCompletion.data.

Key constraint: ToolCall/ToolResult events already use `data.schema = agent_api.tools.structured.v1`; obs metadata must coexist without breaking the tools facet contract (likely via an optional nested `obs` object in `data`, not a competing top-level schema for tool events).
Steps:
1) Specify the obs object shape + strict bounds (identifiers + small maps only; no raw backend lines).
2) Specify where the obs data MAY appear (which event kinds, completion.data) and merge/precedence rules when both caller and backend supply context.
3) Specify how this schema relates to upcoming surfaces: agent_api.obs.run_id.v1, agent_api.obs.trace_context.v1, agent_api.obs.tags.v1.
Output: spec text updates ready for implementation (keep it conservative and backward-compatible with existing tools facet rules).
deliverables: A concrete obs facet schema (v1) + placement rules + bounds, suitable to unblock run_id/trace_context/tags work

notes: Define a single bounded obs facet schema carried in AgentWrapperEvent.data and/or AgentWrapperCompletion.data (AgentWrapperEvent has no top-level run/session/trace fields).
This facet should be the foundation for: agent_api.obs.run_id.v1, agent_api.obs.trace_context.v1, agent_api.obs.tags.v1.
Decide emission model: always emit vs gated by explicit capabilities and/or an extension key; define precedence + merge rules when both backend and caller supply context.
Draft spec text (backlog-only): docs/backlog/uaa-0014-obs-facet-schema-v1.md
On hold. This is not a priority right now.

items.title: Research/plan: backend config pass-through (bounded)

prompt: Work item: uaa-0012 — Research/plan: backend config pass-through (bounded).

Goal: do a final sweep of codex + claude_code flags/settings/config surfaces and decide what becomes universal (`agent_api.config.*` or other agent_api.* keys) vs what stays backend-specific (bounded pass-through under backend.<agent_kind>.*).
Steps:
1) Inventory: extract global flags + exec/print flags + key=value config mechanisms from cli_manifests/*/current.json and wrapper_coverage manifests.
2) Normalize by semantics: model selection, context roots, safety/permissions, tool allow/deny, output/artifacts, session selectors, MCP, etc.
3) Promote only backend-neutral semantics into agent_api.*; everything else becomes one or more bounded backend pass-through keys with strict validation/bounds.
Output: a concrete proposed key list (universal keys + backend pass-through keys), including JSON schema sketches, defaults, bounds, and validation rules.
deliverables: Concrete list of proposed universal config keys (agent_api.config.*)
Concrete list of bounded backend pass-through keys (backend.codex.* / backend.claude_code.*) with schema/bounds

notes: Final pass before implementation: inventory codex + claude_code global flags, key=value config surfaces, and settings inputs (via cli_manifests/*/current.json and wrapper coverage manifests).
Promote only backend-neutral semantics into agent_api.config.* (and/or other universal buckets); keep backend-specific knobs under backend.<agent_kind>.* even if they are 'config'.

Produce a concrete list of bounded backend pass-through keys we want to support (e.g. backend.codex.cli_overrides.v1, backend.claude_code.settings.v1), including schema, defaults, bounds, and validation rules.

items.title: Universal: external sandbox execution policy (dangerous)
notes: Expose an explicit, capability-gated core key for hosts (e.g. Substrate) that provide isolation externally and need the CLI backend to relax internal guardrails.
Owned by docs/specs/unified-agent-api/extensions-spec.md (core key); implement + capability-gate in both codex and claude_code backends.

When true, the backend MUST remain non-interactive (MUST NOT hang on prompts); if agent_api.exec.non_interactive is explicitly false, the backend MUST fail before spawn with AgentWrapperError::InvalidRequest (contradictory intent).
This key is explicitly dangerous and SHOULD NOT be advertised by default in built-in backends (only enabled for externally sandboxed hosts).
Value MUST be boolean and validated before spawn; unknown keys must continue to fail-closed.

codex.impl_hint: crates/agent_api/src/backends/codex.rs: when true, configure CodexClientBuilder::dangerously_bypass_approvals_and_sandbox(true) (or equivalent never-prompt + danger-full-access policy).

claude_code.impl_hint: crates/agent_api/src/backends/claude_code.rs: when true, map to ClaudePrintRequest::dangerously_skip_permissions(true) (and enable allow_dangerously_skip_permissions when required by the CLI version).

items.title: Add typed session/thread id accessors (codex + claude_code)

prompt: Work item: uaa-0017 — Add typed session/thread id accessors (codex + claude_code).
Goal: add small helper accessors on the typed backend event models so downstream crates can extract a stable session/thread identifier without duplicating match logic.
Steps:
1) Codex: add `codex::ThreadEvent::thread_id() -> Option<&str>` (or equivalent) to return the best-effort thread id when the variant contains one.
2) Claude Code: add `claude_code::ClaudeStreamJsonEvent::session_id() -> Option<&str>` to return the best-effort session id (including the Unknown variant when present).
3) Use these helpers in both `wrapper_events` and `agent_api` (uaa-0015) so session handle extraction is implemented once per backend type.
Output: ergonomic, test-covered accessors; no contract/spec changes.
deliverables: Typed accessor helpers for session/thread ids, reused by wrapper_events and agent_api

notes: This is a small reuse seam: avoid duplicating per-backend match logic in multiple crates.
Intended as a prerequisite for uaa-0015 (session handle facet emission).

items.title: Session/thread id surfacing (for resume-by-id UX)

prompt: Work item: uaa-0015 — Session/thread id surfacing (for resume-by-id UX).
Goal: expose the backend’s session/thread identifier to the caller as stable, bounded metadata so resume-by-id is ergonomic (see ADR-0017).
Inputs already exist via streaming/log parsing:
- Codex: ThreadEvent::ThreadStarted.thread_id (thread.started/thread.resumed).
- Claude: stream-json session_id (SystemInit and other events).
Steps:
1) Decision: capability id `agent_api.session.handle.v1`; emit a bounded facet in both an early Status event `data` payload and in `AgentWrapperCompletion.data` (schema pinned in ADR-0017).
2) Update canonical specs to register the capability + facet schema/emission rules:
- docs/specs/unified-agent-api/capabilities-schema-spec.md
- docs/specs/unified-agent-api/event-envelope-schema-spec.md
3) Prerequisite: land uaa-0017 typed accessors and use them for extraction (`codex::ThreadEvent::thread_id()`, `claude_code::ClaudeStreamJsonEvent::session_id()`).
4) Implement extraction + run-local storage in both agent_api backends (Claude: capture session_id from stream-json; Codex: capture thread_id from JSONL ThreadStarted).
5) Advertise `agent_api.session.handle.v1` only once extraction + bounds/redaction posture is implemented + tested.
Output: cross-backend consistent session/thread id surfacing + tests (fake-binary where appropriate).
deliverables: Stable session/thread id payload surfaced to callers (schema + implementation + tests)

notes: Make session/thread identifiers discoverable to callers as a stable, bounded payload (so resume-by-id can be ergonomic).
Leverage existing streaming/log parsing: Codex exposes thread_id in thread.started/thread.resumed events; Claude exposes session_id in stream-json events.

Decision: when capability `agent_api.session.handle.v1` is advertised, emit the handle facet once in an early Status event `data` payload and attach it to `AgentWrapperCompletion.data` when a completion is produced and the id is known.
Prerequisite: uaa-0017 adds typed id accessors so extraction logic is not duplicated across crates.
Use stream-json parsing to extract session_id (per docs/specs/claude-stream-json-parser-contract.md) and surface it in a stable payload.
Use JSONL parsing to extract ThreadEvent::ThreadStarted.thread_id (thread.started/thread.resumed) and surface it in a stable payload.

items.title: P1: Restrict selection-failure translation to actual not-found errors

prompt: Work item: uaa-0018 — P1: Restrict selection-failure translation to actual not-found errors.

Goal: ensure resume/fork selection failures map to pinned safe messages only when the backend truly indicates a not-found condition; do not mislabel unrelated backend failures as "no session found" / "session not found".
Primary impact: Claude Code backend (agent_api) currently infers selection failure from non-success exit + no assistant output and suppresses ResultError events, masking real failure causes.
Steps:
1) Adjust Claude backend selection-failure detection to rely on explicit not-found signals (e.g., structured ResultError payload or stable not-found indicator), not absence-of-output.
2) Ensure non-not-found failures surface as AgentWrapperError::Backend with a safe/redacted message and emit a single terminal Error event when events are available.
3) Add regression tests: generic resume/fork failure with no assistant output must NOT translate to the pinned selection-failure messages.
Output: correct error classification + pinned selection failures + tests; no backend secrets leak.
deliverables: Selection-failure translation restricted to actual not-found signals (Claude Code)
Regression tests preventing false-positive "session not found" translations

notes: Do not infer selection failure solely from non-zero exit + no assistant output (false positives).
If ResultError events are suppressed for safety, still use their typed/raw payload for classification, and surface a generic safe error for non-not-found failures.

items.title: Beware: Claude stream-json result subtype drift when promoting versions

prompt: Work item: uaa-0019 — Beware: Claude stream-json result subtype drift when promoting versions.

Context: A newer local Claude CLI (e.g., 2.1.63) emitted `type:"result"` with a non-v1 `subtype` (e.g., "error_during_execution") for resume-by-id not-found. This does not parse as `ClaudeStreamJsonEvent::ResultError` under the current v1 parser contract (success/error only), which can break downstream selection-failure translation that relies on ResultError.raw classification.

Goal: when raising Claude Code coverage and/or promoting `cli_manifests/claude_code/latest_validated.txt`, validate stream-json result taxonomy against the candidate binary and update the parser contract + fixtures as needed before promotion.
Steps:
1) Run stream-json spot checks against the candidate `claude` binary (not whatever happens to be on PATH) for: resume id not-found, resume last empty, generic failure.

2) If result subtypes differ from v1 (success/error), update `crates/claude_code` stream-json parser + the normative parser specs/fixtures to accept the new subtype(s) (while keeping safety/redaction posture).
3) Re-validate `agent_api` selection-failure translation against the candidate binary once the parser emits typed ResultError events again.
Output: promotion-safe parser contract updates (specs + fixtures + code) that keep higher Claude versions from silently regressing session error translation.
deliverables: Updated Claude stream-json parser contract/fixtures for new result subtype(s)
Evidence the candidate Claude version preserves typed ResultError emission for agent_api

notes: Avoid validating against an arbitrary local `claude` on PATH; use the binary under promotion and compare to cli_manifests pointers.
If new result subtypes appear, treat it as a promotion gate: update parser contract + fixtures before bumping latest_validated/min_supported.

items.title: P2: Enforce 1024-byte handle id bound for Codex fork thread IDs

prompt: Work item: uaa-0020 — P2: Enforce 1024-byte handle id bound for Codex fork thread IDs.
Goal: ensure the Codex fork path treats oversize thread ids (>1024 bytes) as unknown (omit session handle facet; SHOULD warn) per the session handle facet contract.

Context: Codex exec/resume path enforces the bound before storing thread_id, but the fork path stores any non-empty id, allowing oversize ids to leak into handle facets via app-server notifications and completion data.
Steps:
1) Update `crates/agent_api/src/backends/codex/fork.rs` to enforce the 1024-byte bound before storing the forked thread id (and treat oversize as unknown).
2) Emit a safe `Status` warning once per run when an oversize id is observed (align with the exec/resume warning marker in `crates/agent_api/src/backends/codex.rs`).

3) Add regression tests by extending the fake codex app-server test binary to return an oversize forked thread id, and assert the handle facet is omitted in both events and completion (and warning emitted if implemented).
Output: consistent bound enforcement across Codex exec + fork paths; no contract violations.
deliverables: Bound enforcement for forked thread ids (<=1024 bytes)
Regression tests for oversize forked thread ids

notes: Contract: ids over 1024 bytes must be treated as unknown (omit facet; MUST NOT truncate); SHOULD warn.
Keep behavior consistent across Codex exec/resume and Codex fork flows.

items.title: Claude: persistent project-scoped state isolation parity (.claude / .mcp.json)

prompt: Work item: uaa-0021 — Claude: persistent project-scoped state isolation parity (.claude / .mcp.json).

Goal: design and implement Codex-like persistent, app-managed project state isolation for Claude runs so project-scoped Claude artifacts are redirected away from the real repo while preserving access to the real workspace.

Context: `claude_home` only isolates user-scoped state (`~/.claude*`). Claude also discovers project-scoped state from the working tree, including `.claude/settings*.json`, `.claude/agents`, `.claude/commands`, hooks/memory files, `CLAUDE.md`, and `.mcp.json`. A stop-gap `agent_api` `claude_home` wiring can ship independently, but it does not solve project-local state isolation.
Steps:

1) Inventory which Claude surfaces are discovered from cwd/project root vs user home, and pin the minimum parity target for v1 (at least `.claude/**`, `.mcp.json`, and `CLAUDE.md` discovery semantics).
2) Design a persistent app-managed project-root layout/overlay in `crates/claude_code` that can host isolated Claude project files while still exposing the real workspace contents to the CLI.

3) Decide how the wrapper selects and materializes that project overlay across repeated runs (persistent per app/project key, not one tempdir per run), including cleanup/update behavior and cross-platform filesystem strategy.

4) Evaluate where existing Claude flags (`--settings`, `--setting-sources`, `--mcp-config`) can be reused for subsets of the problem versus where project-root overlay behavior is still required for parity.

5) Expose the new project-state isolation controls through `crates/claude_code` first, then add host-facing `agent_api` config + canonical spec text only once the behavior is implemented and testable.
6) Add end-to-end tests proving Claude reads/writes isolated `.claude` / `.mcp.json` content under the app-managed root rather than the real repo, without regressing normal workspace file access.
Output: a concrete crate/API design plus implementation plan for full Claude project-state isolation; keep the smaller `claude_home` stop-gap tracked separately and unblocked.
deliverables: A persistent project-overlay design for Claude project-scoped state
An implementation plan for exposing the feature in claude_code and then agent_api
A test strategy covering isolated `.claude` and `.mcp.json` behavior

notes: Do not treat `claude_home` as sufficient parity: it isolates user-home state, not project-local `.claude` / `.mcp.json` discovery.
Prefer a persistent app-managed directory keyed per app/project over ephemeral tempdirs so repeated runs share the same isolated Claude project state.
Likely requires new `claude_code` crate primitives first; `agent_api` should only surface the config after the lower-level behavior exists.
The stop-gap `ClaudeCodeBackendConfig.claude_home` wiring can land independently and should not be blocked on this larger feature.
Needs new crate support for a persistent project overlay/root that redirects project-scoped Claude discovery (`.claude/**`, `.mcp.json`, `CLAUDE.md`) while preserving access to the real workspace.
Serves as the parity reference for app-managed persistent state isolation, but Codex's native `CODEX_HOME` model is user-home scoped and does not directly solve Claude's project-root discovery model.

items.title: Non-interactive execution policy
notes: Intent: do not hang on interactive permission/approval prompts in headless automation.
Owned by docs/specs/unified-agent-api/extensions-spec.md.

items.title: Universal model selection
notes: Expose model selection behind a core key shared by codex and claude_code.
Define schema/defaults in docs/specs/unified-agent-api/extensions-spec.md, then implement + gate in both backends.

items.title: Universal add-dir / extra context roots
notes: Provide a single cross-agent way to add extra context directories for tool access / indexing / file reads.
Value should be a bounded list of paths; decide absolute vs relative semantics in the core spec.

items.title: Universal resume: selector=last
notes: Resume the most recent session/thread in the working dir (backend-defined persistence store).
Keep agent_api.run prompt non-empty; this is 'resume + send follow-up prompt', not 'resume with no new prompt'.

items.title: Universal resume: selector=id
notes: Resume a specific session/thread by identifier.
Claude uses UUID session ids; Codex accepts UUID or thread name (UUID wins if it parses).

items.title: Universal MCP management commands
notes: Unify a minimal MCP management surface (add/get/list/remove) across backends.
May require a non-run API (not AgentWrapperRunRequest.extensions), since these are CLI subcommands.
Claude also has mcp add-json, add-from-claude-desktop, serve, reset-project-choices (backend-specific).
Codex add supports --url vs -- <COMMAND...>; Claude add uses <commandOrUrl> [args...] with different flag shapes.

items.title: Universal fork session
notes: Expose a cross-agent 'fork conversation' operation when both CLIs support it in headless mode.
Codex mapping MUST use a headless surface; prefer `codex app-server` JSON-RPC (`thread/fork` + `turn/start`) over any interactive/TUI `codex fork` flow.

codex.impl_hint: crates/codex::mcp: launch app-server + call thread/fork + turn/start (or equivalent typed client)

items.title: Obs: stable run_id emission
notes: Expose a stable per-run id for correlation across events + completion.
Contract constraint: AgentWrapperEvent has no run_id field, so this must be expressed via AgentWrapperEvent.data (facet schema) and/or completion.data.
Decide whether run_id is always generated (recommended) vs only present when explicitly requested via an extension key.
No upstream CLI flag; wrapper/agent_api would generate and attach metadata.
No upstream CLI flag; wrapper/agent_api would generate and attach metadata.

items.title: Obs: trace context propagation
notes: Allow callers to provide trace context that is attached to events and (when possible) propagated to the underlying backend process.
Define a concrete schema (e.g. {traceparent,tracestate,baggage} or a single opaque string) and explicit bounds in the core extensions spec.
Potential mapping: env var injection for subprocess + event facets (no known upstream flag).
Potential mapping: env var injection for subprocess + event facets (no known upstream flag).

items.title: Obs: tags / key-value annotations
notes: Allow callers to attach bounded tags (e.g., workflow ids, customer ids, repo ids) to every event and completion.
Prefer a small bounded string map {k: v} with strict limits on count and bytes.
No upstream CLI flag; wrapper/agent_api would attach metadata; optional propagation via env.
No upstream CLI flag; wrapper/agent_api would attach metadata; optional propagation via env.

items.title: Session: consolidate universal session controls
notes: Introduce `agent_api.session.*` as a first-class bucket for conversation/thread semantics (orthogonal to execution policy).

Decision: keep separate core extension keys (`agent_api.session.resume.v1`, `agent_api.session.fork.v1`) rather than a single consolidated `agent_api.session.v1` object; see ADR-0015 and the canonical `extensions-spec.md` registry.
Bucket + tooling support for `agent_api.session.*` is tracked in uaa-0013 (now done).
Session-related flags live on the headless --print surface: --continue, --resume, --session-id, --fork-session.
Session-related commands exist as non-interactive subcommands: codex exec resume, codex resume, codex fork.
